close_reason: Merged into US04 (workspace-bms.1.2) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
The clarified_into_id column on inbox_item table is missing REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT foreign key constraint, allowing orphaned clarification references.

Fix:
Add REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT to the clarified_into_id column in the D1 migration for inbox_item table.
title: [security] Missing FK constraint on clarified_into_id column

close_reason: Merged into US02 (workspace-bms.1.1) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
Content Security Policy blocks inline event handlers including hx-on::after-settle. Use Alpine.js x-on:htmx:after-settle.window attribute instead.

Fix:
Replace all hx-on::after-settle= attributes in templates with Alpine.js x-on:htmx:after-settle.window= equivalents.
title: [security] CSP blocks hx-on::after-settle — use Alpine.js x-on:htmx:after-settle.window

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (open redirect mitigation, safeRedirectTo implementation)
Severity: Low

Problem:
Proposed regex does not strip CR or LF. A redirectTo value containing CRLF characters (e.g., /path%0D%0ASet-Cookie:evil=1) passes the regex and, if set as a Location header, enables HTTP response header injection in non-Workers execution contexts (tests, local dev, future Node.js adapters).

Fix:
Add if (/[\r\n]/.test(value)) return fallback; before the path-shape check. Cloudflare Workers mitigates this at runtime, but the utility should be safe-by-construction.
title: [security] safeRedirectTo missing CRLF sanitization

close_reason: Merged into US03 (workspace-bms.1.4) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/data-model.md (InboxItemRepository vs D1InboxItemRepository)
Severity: High

Problem:
Interface declares listByWorkspaceId(workspaceId: string) with no status param, but the planned D1 implementation adds status: InboxItemStatus = 'inbox' — the concrete class will not satisfy the interface and TypeScript will error.

Fix:
Update the domain interface to listByWorkspaceId(workspaceId: string, status?: InboxItemStatus): Promise<InboxItem[]>.
title: [architecture] InboxItemRepository interface mismatch with planned D1 implementation

close_reason: Closed
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md:219
Severity: Critical

Problem:
plan.md references acceptance spec files specs/acceptance-specs/US01-workspace-provisioning.spec (and US03-US05) as deliverables, but none of these files exist in the PR.

Fix:
Create GWT-format spec files for US01, US03, US04, US05 in specs/acceptance-specs/, or explicitly state these are out of scope for this PR and track them as separate tasks.
title: [quality] Acceptance spec files referenced in plan.md do not exist

close_reason: Implemented requireWorkspace middleware with explicit actor null check returning 503. Created WorkspaceRepository and ActorRepository interfaces, updated AppEnv.Variables with workspaceId and actorId, and added 9 tests covering all security paths.
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Critical

Problem:
requireWorkspace middleware must return 503 when actor ID is empty — falling through with empty string produces silent data corruption (audit events with empty actor_id, actions owned by nobody).

Fix:
Add explicit check: if (!actorId) return c.json({ error: 'Workspace not provisioned' }, 503);
title: [security] Empty actor ID in requireWorkspace must 503, not fall through

description:
File: contracts/openapi.yaml (entire file)
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Missing components.securitySchemes and security: declarations — auth requirement is prose-only. SDK generators and automated security scanners will treat all endpoints as unauthenticated, producing client code that omits credentials.

Fix:
Add components.securitySchemes.cookieAuth (type: apiKey, in: cookie, name: better-auth.session_token) and add security: [{cookieAuth: []}] to all operations.
title: [security] Missing securitySchemes and security declarations in OpenAPI

close_reason: Merged into US01 (workspace-bms.1.3) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
WorkspaceProvisioningService must use .bind() for all external values in D1 queries. Direct string interpolation in SQL statements is vulnerable to injection.

Fix:
Replace all string-interpolated D1 queries with parameterized statements using .bind(...params).
title: [security] SQL injection — WorkspaceProvisioningService must use .bind()

close_reason: Merged into US04 (workspace-bms.1.2) as implementation constraints
description:
File: contracts/openapi.yaml:273
Severity: High

Problem:
POST /inbox/{id}/clarify response body { inboxItem, action } is not defined as a DTO anywhere in data-model.md — implementation must invent the response shape ad hoc.

Fix:
Add ClarifyInboxItemResultDto to the data-model.md DTOs section matching the { inboxItem, action } OpenAPI response shape.
title: [quality] ClarifyInboxItemResultDto not defined in data-model.md

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
When HX-Request: true, requireAuth must return HX-Redirect header instead of a 302 redirect — HTMX will otherwise swap the login page HTML into the current target element instead of navigating.

Fix:
In requireAuth middleware, check if HX-Request header is present; if so, return 200 with HX-Redirect: /login header instead of Response.redirect().
title: [security] HTMX session expiry — requireAuth must return HX-Redirect

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Without a global app.onError() handler, unhandled exceptions in Hono will expose internal error details (stack traces, SQL errors) to clients.

Fix:
Add app.onError((err, c) => c.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, 500)) to the Hono app setup; log the error internally.
title: [security] No global app.onError() — unhandled exceptions expose internals

close_reason: Merged into US03 (workspace-bms.1.4) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md vs plan.md:444
Severity: Critical

Problem:
GET /api/v1/inbox behavior is undefined — spec says list of workspace inbox items with no status filter, but plan silently filters to status='inbox' only with no way to retrieve clarified items.

Fix:
Update spec.md acceptance scenario for US02 to explicitly state the filter applied, or add a ?status= query param to the endpoint (matching GET /actions).
title: [quality] GET /api/v1/inbox filter behavior undefined in spec

close_reason: Merged into US01 (workspace-bms.1.3) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/data-model.md:382
Severity: High

Problem:
AuditEvent.actor_id TEXT NOT NULL constraint has no specified value for system-generated provisioning events (workspace, areas, contexts created before the human actor exists).

Fix:
Document whether provisioning audit events use the newly created human actor ID, a synthetic system actor ID, or a nullable override of the constraint.
title: [quality] AuditEvent.actor_id value undefined for provisioning events

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Calling req.json() without first validating Content-Type: application/json can cause parse errors or unexpected behavior with malformed requests.

Fix:
Add Content-Type validation middleware or check before all req.json() calls in Hono route handlers.
title: [security] Content-Type header must be validated before req.json()

description: Full vertical slice: workspace auto-provisioning, inbox capture, clarify to action, activate, complete — API + HTMX UI. Branch: 012-clawtask-vertical-slice. Spec: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md
title: Feature: ClawTask Vertical Slice — Inbox Capture to Completed Action

description: Execute all implementation tasks for the ClawTask vertical slice. Parent task for all user story tasks and sub-tasks. See plan.md for implementation details.
title: [sp:07-implement] Implement feature

description:
**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US02
**Goal**: Users can perform the full inbox→clarify→activate→complete flow through a web interface without API calls. Three pages: Dashboard (/app), Inbox (/app/inbox), Actions (/app/actions).
**Acceptance**: Dashboard shows inbox count + active count + Quick Capture form. Capture submits without full reload. Clarify shows inline form with area/context selects. Actions page with Activate/Complete buttons updating inline. Full flow without page reload.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[High] CSP blocks hx-on::after-settle — use Alpine.js x-on:htmx:after-settle.window** (workspace-1ea)
Do not use hx-on::after-settle= attributes in any template. Content Security Policy blocks inline event handlers. Use Alpine.js x-on:htmx:after-settle.window= equivalents instead.

**[High] All HTMX mutation handlers must validate _csrf against __Host-csrf cookie** (workspace-kzv)
Implement CSRF middleware that reads the __Host-csrf cookie and compares it to the _csrf form field. Apply to all state-changing Hono routes (POST, PUT, DELETE). Missing validation enables CSRF attacks.

**[High] All templates must use html tagged template literal — no raw string concatenation** (workspace-mwz)
All HTML templates in src/presentation/templates/ must use html`...` (or the project's escaping utility) for any user-supplied values. Plain string interpolation into HTML is XSS-vulnerable.

**[High] User-specific HTML responses must set Cache-Control: no-store** (workspace-cpy)
All authenticated page responses (inbox, actions, workspace pages) must include Cache-Control: no-store header to prevent sensitive user data from being cached by CDN or browser.
title: US02: HTMX Web UI

Requires workspaceId from requireWorkspace middleware. 100% test coverage.

title: Wire AppPage and AppPartial handlers into DI and register /app/* routes in src/worker.ts

description:
**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US04
**Goal**: A user can process an inbox item and turn it into a concrete, actionable task by assigning it an Area and Context. Atomic D1 batch: inbox item → clarified, new action → ready.
**Acceptance**: POST /api/v1/inbox/:id/clarify with areaId+contextId → inbox item status=clarified + new action status=ready + 2 audit events. Already-clarified item → 422. Invalid area → 422. Invalid context → 422.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] ClarifyInboxItemToAction: use optimistic lock via WHERE status='inbox'** (workspace-gib)
Implement atomic conditional UPDATE: UPDATE inbox_item SET status='clarified' WHERE id=? AND status='inbox'; check rows affected = 1 before proceeding with action creation. Without this, concurrent clarify requests cause a TOCTOU race producing double-clarification.

**[High] Clarify request: description is an optional override, not required** (workspace-g0u)
The action inherits the inbox item's description automatically. The POST /api/v1/inbox/:id/clarify request body may accept an optional description field to override the inherited value. Update FR-002 and openapi.yaml to make this explicit.

**[High] Define ClarifyInboxItemResultDto { inboxItem, action } in data-model.md** (workspace-40y)
Add ClarifyInboxItemResultDto to the DTOs section of data-model.md matching the { inboxItem, action } shape already in openapi.yaml. The response shape must not be invented ad hoc at implementation time.

**[High] Migration: add clarified_into_id FK with REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT** (workspace-089)
The clarified_into_id column on inbox_item table must have REFERENCES action(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT to prevent orphaned clarification references. Include this constraint in the D1 migration for the inbox_item table.
title: US04: Clarify Inbox Item into Standalone Action

description:
**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US04, plan.md §Key Design Decisions §2
**Skills**: /cloudflare-use-case-creator, /d1-repository-implementation, /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/application/use-cases/ClarifyInboxItemToActionUseCase.ts, ClarifyInboxItemToActionUseCase.spec.ts, src/application/dto/ActionDto.ts
**Acceptance**: ClarifyInboxItemToActionUseCase.execute({workspaceId, actorId, inboxItemId, title, areaId, contextId}) — validates inbox item exists + status=inbox, validates area exists + status=active, validates context exists, calls clarify() on inbox item, creates Action, records 2 audit events (inbox_item.clarified + action.created). Uses D1 batch (env.DB.batch) for atomicity — NOT Drizzle transactions (not supported). Returns {inboxItem: InboxItemDto, action: ActionDto}. 422 on invalid transitions or missing refs. 100% test coverage.
title: Create ClarifyInboxItemToAction use case (D1 batch atomic)

description:
**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US01
**Goal**: When a new user signs up, their workspace, actor identity, and default seed data (3 Areas + 5 Contexts) are automatically created before the user ever reaches the application.
**Acceptance**: Register a new account, immediately hit any authenticated endpoint, verify workspace + actor + areas + contexts all exist. GET /api/v1/areas returns 3 seeded areas. GET /api/v1/contexts returns 5 seeded contexts.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] requireWorkspace: empty actorId must return 503, not fall through** (workspace-35c)
Add explicit null guard: if (!actorId) return c.json({ error: 'Workspace not provisioned' }, 503); — falling through with empty string produces silent data corruption (audit events with empty actor_id, actions owned by nobody).

**[Critical] D1 batch FK ordering: audit events must be inserted last** (workspace-svs)
Order D1 batch statements: 1) workspace, 2) actor, 3) areas+contexts, 4) audit events. Audit events have FKs on workspace_id and actor_id which must exist first or the batch fails with FK constraint errors.

**[High] All D1 statements must use .bind() — no string interpolation** (workspace-40o)
WorkspaceProvisioningService must use parameterized statements (.bind(...params)) for all external values. Direct string interpolation in SQL is a SQL injection vulnerability.

**[High] Provisioning audit events: actor_id must be the newly created human actor ID** (workspace-85o)
Use the newly created human actor's ID for all provisioning audit events (workspace, areas, contexts). Do not leave actor_id empty or use a synthetic system actor.

**[High] WorkspaceProvisioningService must be a thin D1-batch adapter called by ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase** (workspace-v4b)
Do not put domain orchestration in WorkspaceProvisioningService. The auth hook calls ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase; the use case calls the service as a D1-batch transport. Business logic stays in the application layer.
title: US01: Workspace Auto-Provisioning on Signup

description:
**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US03
**Goal**: A user can instantly capture a thought/task/commitment to their inbox without upfront categorisation. POST /api/v1/inbox creates inbox item. GET /api/v1/inbox lists all workspace inbox items.
**Acceptance**: POST with title → 201 + inbox item with status=inbox + audit event. POST with empty title → 400. GET → list of workspace inbox items.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[High] InboxItemRepository interface must accept optional status filter** (workspace-2bk)
Define listByWorkspaceId(workspaceId: string, status?: InboxItemStatus): Promise<InboxItem[]> in the domain interface. Without the optional param the concrete D1 class will not satisfy the interface and TypeScript will error.

**[Critical] GET /api/v1/inbox filters to status='inbox' only — document this explicitly** (workspace-7tc)
The plan silently filters to status='inbox' only, but the spec says 'list of workspace inbox items' with no filter. Explicitly state in the acceptance criteria that GET /api/v1/inbox returns only status='inbox' items (clarified items are not shown). Accepted decision: filter to inbox status only, matching the plan.

**[Medium] Validate Content-Type: application/json before calling req.json()** (workspace-89u)
Add Content-Type header validation (or a Hono middleware) before all req.json() calls in InboxApiHandlers to avoid parse errors and unexpected behavior with malformed requests.
title: US03: Capture to Inbox

description:

**Skills**: /ddd-domain-modeling, /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/domain/entities/InboxItem.ts, src/domain/entities/InboxItem.spec.ts
**Acceptance**: InboxItem.create(workspaceId, title, description?) validates title 1-255 chars, generates UUID+timestamps, sets status='inbox'. clarify(actionId) returns new InboxItem with status='clarified', clarifiedIntoType='action', clarifiedIntoId=actionId, updated updatedAt — throws DomainError('invalid_status_transition') if already clarified. reconstitute(row) hydrates from D1. Error codes: 'title_required', 'title_too_long', 'description_too_long', 'invalid_status_transition'. 100% test coverage.
title: Create InboxItem domain entity in src/domain/entities/InboxItem.ts

description:
**Spec**: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md §US05
**Goal**: A user can move an action through its lifecycle: ready → active → done. Each transition recorded as audit event. GET /api/v1/actions lists actions (optional ?status= filter).
**Acceptance**: POST activate on ready action → active + audit event. POST complete on active → done + audit event. POST activate on done → 422. POST complete on ready → 422.

---
## Implementation Constraints (merged from remediation tasks)

**[Critical] No raw D1 SQL in use cases — state transitions via ActionRepository methods** (workspace-uku)
ActivateActionUseCase and CompleteActionUseCase must NOT call db.prepare()/stmt.run() directly. The application layer must never import infrastructure. Add activateById and completeById to ActionRepository in src/domain/interfaces/ and implement the conditional UPDATE in D1ActionRepository.

**[High] ActionRepository interface: add activateById and completeById returning Promise<boolean>** (workspace-yar)
Add activateById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> and completeById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> to the ActionRepository interface in src/domain/interfaces/ActionRepository.ts. Return true if the row was updated, false if not found or wrong state (enables 422 detection in use case).

**[High] Validate ?status= query param against enum before SQL — return 400 for invalid values** (workspace-v6l)
The ?status= parameter in GET /api/v1/actions must be validated against ['ready','active','done'] before use in D1 query. Absent param = return all statuses. Empty string or invalid value = 400. Do not pass unvalidated strings into SQL.
title: US05: Activate and Complete a Standalone Action

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
All user-specific HTML responses (inbox, actions, workspace pages) must include Cache-Control: no-store to prevent sensitive data from being cached by CDN or browser.

Fix:
Add Cache-Control: no-store header to all authenticated page responses in Hono route handlers.
title: [security] Cache-Control: no-store missing from user-specific HTML responses

close_reason: Closed
description:
File: contracts/openapi.yaml:71 / specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/data-model.md:413
Severity: Critical

Problem:
ActionDto lists createdByActorId as required in data-model.md (line 413) but the Action schema in OpenAPI omits it entirely. Implementations will inconsistently expose or hide the field.

Fix:
Add createdByActorId to the OpenAPI Action schema in contracts/openapi.yaml, or remove it from the ActionDto definition in data-model.md.
title: [quality] createdByActorId missing from OpenAPI Action schema

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/data-model.md
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Context entity has no updatedAt field while Area does — immutability of Context is never stated, leaving it ambiguous whether this is intentional design or an omission.

Fix:
Add one sentence to the Context entity description: Context records are immutable seed data; no updatedAt field is stored.
title: [quality] Context entity immutability not stated (missing updatedAt)

close_reason: Merged into US04 (workspace-bms.1.2) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/spec.md:123 (FR-002) vs contracts/openapi.yaml:250
Severity: High

Problem:
Clarification request schema accepts only title, areaId, contextId — spec says action inherits inbox item description automatically, but never clarifies whether the request can override description, leaving handlers no guidance.

Fix:
Specify in FR-002 whether the clarify request accepts an optional description override; update openapi.yaml request body accordingly.
title: [quality] Clarify request body ambiguity re: description override

close_reason: Merged into US04 (workspace-bms.1.2) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Critical

Problem:
Clarification action has TOCTOU race condition — needs optimistic lock via WHERE status = 'inbox' in the UPDATE statement to prevent double-clarification of the same inbox item.

Fix:
Implement atomic conditional UPDATE: UPDATE inbox_item SET status='clarified' WHERE id=? AND status='inbox'; check rows affected = 1 before proceeding.
title: [security] TOCTOU race on clarification — missing optimistic lock

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/research.md:11 + plan.md:233
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Plan states databaseHooks.user.create.after is confirmed available in better-auth v1.4.x but no version check or source is cited; fallback pattern is documented but unfollowed.

Fix:
Record which installed version was checked, or add verify hook availability as the first implementation step with the fallback keyed to a specific failure condition.
title: [quality] better-auth databaseHooks version not verified

close_reason: Merged into US02 (workspace-bms.1.1) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
All HTMX mutation endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE) must validate the _csrf form field against the __Host-csrf cookie to prevent cross-site request forgery.

Fix:
Implement CSRF middleware that reads __Host-csrf cookie and compares to _csrf form field; apply to all state-changing Hono routes.
title: [security] CSRF — HTMX mutation endpoints must validate _csrf token

close_reason: Merged into US02 (workspace-bms.1.1) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
All HTML templates must use the html tagged template literal to auto-escape user content. Templates using plain string interpolation are vulnerable to XSS.

Fix:
Ensure all template files in src/presentation/templates/ use html`...` (or equivalent escaping utility) for any user-supplied values.
title: [security] XSS — all templates must use html tagged template literal

close_reason: test issue
description: test description
title: [quality] test

description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Medium

Problem:
The safeRedirectTo utility must be applied to the post-login redirect in AuthPageHandlers to prevent open redirect attacks via the redirectTo parameter.

Fix:
Apply safeRedirectTo() to all redirect-after-login flows in AuthPageHandlers.
title: [security] Open redirect in safeRedirectTo must be applied to AuthPageHandlers

close_reason: Closed
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: Critical

Problem:
Audit events in the D1 batch must be inserted last — foreign keys on workspace_id, actor_id, etc. must exist before audit events reference them, or the batch will fail with FK constraint errors.

Fix:
Order D1 batch statements: 1) workspace, 2) actor, 3) areas+contexts, 4) audit events. Verify FK ordering in WorkspaceProvisioningService.
title: [security] Audit event FK ordering in provisioning D1 batch

close_reason: Merged into US05 (workspace-bms.1.5) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md:858-865
Severity: Critical

Problem:
ActivateActionUseCase and CompleteActionUseCase are planned to call db.prepare() / stmt.run() directly — raw D1 SQL in the application layer violates the dependency rule (application must never import infrastructure).

Fix:
Add activateById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> and completeById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> to ActionRepository in src/domain/interfaces/; implement the conditional UPDATE there in D1ActionRepository.
title: [architecture] Raw D1 SQL in application layer (ActivateAction/CompleteAction)

close_reason: Merged into US01 (workspace-bms.1.3) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md:154,438,855
Severity: High

Problem:
WorkspaceProvisioningService at src/infrastructure/ contains domain orchestration (workspace + actor + seed areas/contexts + audit events) and bypasses all repository interfaces; the auth hook calls it directly, never invoking ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase. Business logic is split across two layers for the same operation.

Fix:
Reduce WorkspaceProvisioningService to a thin D1-batch adapter; have it called by ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase rather than parallel to it.
title: [architecture] WorkspaceProvisioningService bypasses ProvisionWorkspaceUseCase

close_reason: Merged into US05 (workspace-bms.1.5) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/plan.md (Security Considerations)
Severity: High

Problem:
The ?status= query parameter in GET /actions is not validated against the allowed enum values, enabling injection of arbitrary status strings into SQL queries.

Fix:
Validate status param against ['ready','active','done'] enum before using in D1 query; return 400 for invalid values.
title: [security] Status query parameter injection — validate against enum

close_reason: Merged into US05 (workspace-bms.1.5) as implementation constraints
description:
File: specs/012-clawtask-vertical-slice/data-model.md (ActionRepository)
Severity: Medium

Problem:
Interface defines only save, getById, listByWorkspaceId, and listByWorkspaceIdAndStatus — no state-transition methods. The Critical finding (raw D1 SQL) requires adding activateById / completeById here to keep the application layer clean.

Fix:
Add both activateById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> and completeById(id: string, workspaceId: string): Promise<boolean> to the ActionRepository interface in src/domain/interfaces/ActionRepository.ts.
title: [architecture] ActionRepository missing state-transition methods
